question.text: For a non-individual investment adviser being a partnership firm, which requirement applies regarding the principal officer?

answer.text: The senior-most partner automatically becomes the principal officer
One of the partners shall be designated as its principal officer
An external professional must be appointed as principal officer
All partners jointly serve as the principal officer

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI IA Regulations specifically state that in case of a non-individual IA being a partnership firm, one of the partners shall be designated as its principal officer. **The Trap:** It's not automatic (the senior-most partner) — a specific designation must be made. Also, it must be a partner, not an external professional. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A and X-B test organizational requirements for different IA entity structures. Series IX covers similar requirements for merchant bankers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If no partner meets the minimum qualification and certification requirements, the firm must convert to an LLP or body corporate within the time specified by SEBI.
